title: HM Amit Shah to chair high-level meeting on flood and heatwave preparedness - News On AIR
full_text:
Union Home Minister Amit Shah will chair a high-level meeting in New Delhi today to review the country’s preparedness for dealing with possible floods and heatwave conditions. During the meeting, Mr Shah will assess the readiness of Central Government agencies, review early warning systems, resource deployment and inter-agency coordination to ensure zero loss of life and minimal loss to property.
The Minister will also review the progress of long-term flood mitigation measures being taken across the country, including protection of critical infrastructure projects, river management initiatives and scientific advancements in forecasting.
Key focus areas include strengthening flood forecasting and early warning systems and enhancing preparedness for dealing with simultaneous disasters, particularly the challenges of floods and heatwaves. Community awareness, resilience-building programmes for preventing loss of life and property and ensuring adequate stock of essential relief materials and medical preparedness will be discussed in the meeting.
This meeting reflects the Government’s commitment to proactive governance, citizen-centric disaster management, and building a disaster-resilient India.

title: Delhi Weather Today: Is there any relief in sight? Here's what the 7-day forecast reveals - Dynamite News
full_text:
New Delhi: Residents of Delhi are expected to experience another sweltering day on Saturday, with the maximum temperature forecast to reach 43 degrees Celsius, while the minimum temperature is likely to remain around 37 degrees Celsius. The weather is expected to stay hot throughout the day, offering little relief from the prevailing heatwave-like conditions.
According to the forecast, the temperature will hover close to 37 degrees Celsius for most of the day. Winds are expected to blow at a speed of around 5.18 km/h, with gusts reaching approximately 8.74 km/h. The wind direction is predicted to remain around 262 degrees, contributing only marginally to cooling conditions.
The sun is scheduled to rise at 5:25 AM and set at 7:22 PM, resulting in long daylight hours and prolonged exposure to intense heat.
The extended weather outlook indicates that Delhi will continue to witness high temperatures over the coming week. The maximum temperature is expected to touch 44 degrees Celsius on Sunday, Monday, and Thursday. On Tuesday and Wednesday, the mercury is likely to remain around 43 degrees Celsius before dipping to 38 degrees Celsius on Friday.
The persistent spell of high temperatures suggests that residents should remain prepared for hot weather conditions and take necessary precautions while venturing outdoors, particularly during the afternoon when heat levels are typically at their peak.
Location : New Delhi
Published : 27 June 2026, 7:49 AM IST
